human_verification.notes: Reviewed the chemicals, plastics, and materials manufacturing process-safety package for specialty boundary quality, authority-source quality, threshold-awareness, startup and hold discipline, incident escalation realism, and validated spec-pack completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to process-safety evidence review, startup and hold handling, threshold-aware applicability posture, and catastrophic-release-risk escalation for chemical, plastics, and materials plants.
Authority sources cover OSHA PSM guidance and enforcement, OSHA inspection targeting, EPA RMP rule text and workflow pages, and process-safety evidence handling with refresh and decay rules.

Evaluation scenarios cover incomplete evidence, restart requests with open holds, threshold gaps, changed field conditions, engineering-signoff refusal, stale audits, policy conflicts, tenant adaptation, and emergency-command boundary rejection.
The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/chemicals-plastics-and-materials-manufacturing/process-safety-specialist/ to preserve the canonical NAICS-derived overlay path.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.
Boundary enforcement remained strong around engineering authority, regulator-facing work, emergency command, and startup release authority.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated chemicals-sector process-safety overlay where engineering, emergency-command, legal, and regulator-facing authority remain external.
Runtime remains specification-based until a retained implementation is separately promoted and committed.
